title: Dynamic Glazing Orientation
summary: Dynamic glazing is most feasible on large glazing facades not facing north, works well on east and west orientations, and can be used efficiently on south facades that are not shaded.
evidence_quote: Dynamic glazing is usually the most feasible on large glazing facades not face north. It works well on east and west orientations since these are difficult to horizontally shade.

title: Desiccant Dehumidification Dew Point Threshold
summary: Applications requiring a dew point at or below 40°F (4.3°C) may be candidates for active desiccant dehumidification.
evidence_quote: applications that require a dew point at or below 40° F (4.3 ° C) may be candidates for active desiccant dehumidification.

title: Desiccant Dehumidification Suitable Facilities
summary: Examples include facilities handling hygroscopic materials, film drying, manufacturing of candy, chocolate, chewing gum, drugs, chemicals, plastics, packaging moisture-sensitive products, electronics, and supermarkets.
evidence_quote: Examples of such candidates include facilities handling hygroscopic materials; film drying; the manufacturing of candy, chocolate, or chewing gum; the manufacturing of drugs and chemicals; the manufacturing of plastic materials; packaging of moisture-sensitive products; and the manufacturing of electronics.

title: Solar Thermal Common Application
summary: The most common solar energy thermal application is for domestic hot water (DHW) production.
evidence_quote: The most common solar energy thermal application is for domestic hot water (DHW) production.

title: Solar Combi Systems
summary: Combined production of DHW and space heating (solar combi systems) includes solar collectors, heat storage tank, and a boiler as auxiliary heater; requires larger collector area than DHW alone.
evidence_quote: A typical installation for the combined production of DHW and space heating (i.e., solar combi systems) includes the solar collectors, the heat storage tank, and a boiler used as an auxiliary heater.

title: Dynamic Glazing Visible Light Transmittance Levels
summary: Typical tint corresponds to 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60% visible light transmittance (Tvis) depending on manufacturer.
evidence_quote: Typically, the tint corresponds to 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60% visible light transmittance Tvis depending on manufacturers.

title: Dynamic Glazing Control
summary: Dynamic glazing is controlled by low voltage current and can be controlled manually through a switch or linked into an automatic building management system (BMS).
evidence_quote: The dynamic glazing is usually controlled by a low voltage current and can be controlled either manually through a switch or linked into an automatic building management system (BMS).

title: Rotary Desiccant Dehumidifier Operation
summary: Rotary desiccant dehumidifiers use solid desiccants like silica gel; humid process air is dehumidified in one part of the bed while another part is dried by reactivation air; the desiccant rotates slowly between airstreams.
evidence_quote: Rotary desiccant dehumidifiers use solid desiccants such as silica gel to attract water vapor from the moist air. Humid air, generally referred to as process air, is dehumidified in one part of the desiccant bed while a different part of the bed is dried for reuse by a second airstream known as reactivation air.

title: Desiccant Regeneration Methods
summary: Desiccant redrying (regeneration) can be either direct (contact with heated outdoor air) or indirect; indirect may be preferable in high humidity climates.
evidence_quote: The redrying can be either direct (by contact with heated outdoor air with the desiccant) or indirect. Indirect may be preferable, particularly in high humid climates, because of the higher temperature needed to maximize the vapor difference and drying potential.

title: Process Air Outlet Temperature Rise
summary: The process air outlet temperature is higher than inlet primarily because the heat of sorption of moisture removed is converted to sensible heat; outlet temperature rises roughly in proportion to moisture removed.
evidence_quote: The process air outlet temperature is higher than the inlet temperature primarily because the heat of sorption of the moisture removed is converted to sensible heat. The outlet temperature rises roughly in proportion to the amount of moisture that is removed.

title: Solar Thermal Applications Temperature Range
summary: Solar thermal applications range from low-temperature (swimming pool heating, DHW) to medium- or high-temperature (space heating, absorption cooling, steam production for electrical generation).
evidence_quote: Solar energy thermal applications range from low-temperature applications (swimming pool heating or domestic hot water) up to medium- or high-temperature applications (space heating, absorption cooling or steam production for electrical generation).

title: Solar PV Economics and Policy
summary: Public policy encourages solar through tax incentives and requiring repurchase of excess electricity; cost for utility-level solar PV is less than new coal plants in the US.
evidence_quote: public policy in many areas encourages solar and other renewable energy applications through tax incentives and encouraging or requiring repurchase of excess electrical energy generated by the utility provider.

title: Desiccant Cooling Supply Air Arrangements
summary: Two supply air arrangements: one uses mixture of recycled return air and dehumidified outdoor ventilation air; the other combines energy recovery from building exhaust air to indirectly cool outdoor air.
evidence_quote: Two supply air arrangements are available. One method uses a mixture of recycled return air and dehumidified outdoor ventilation air as supply air to the building.

title: Desiccant Cooling Energy Recovery
summary: By dehumidifying exhaust air to a sufficiently low humidity ratio, it can be used to indirectly cool outdoor air for supply that has not contacted desiccant; can process up to 100% outside air.
evidence_quote: By dehumidifying the exhaust air to a sufficiently low humidity ratio (i.e., moisture content), it can be used to indirectly cool outdoor air for supply to the building that has not contacted desiccant.

title: Solar Swimming Pool Heating Dominance
summary: In North America, swimming pool heating is dominant with installed capacity of about 55 × 10^9 Btu/h (16 GWth) of unglazed plastic collectors.
evidence_quote: In North America (i.e., United States and Canada), swimming pool heating is dominant with an installed capacity of about 55 × 10^9 Btu/h (16 GWth) of unglazed plastic collectors.
